steps.title: Identify the unexpected finding
steps.content: The passage establishes that social animals with parental care show innate face preference in newborns. But tortoises are solitary and don't care for young—yet their hatchlings also showed significant preference for face-like stimuli.

steps.title: Apply the finding to the choices
steps.content:
If the trait appeared only in social/parental species, you'd assume it evolved specifically for those functions. But tortoises are solitary with no parental care, yet their hatchlings show the same innate preference—so the trait cannot necessarily be explained as an adaptation for social interaction or parental care. Choice B captures this: researchers should not assume the attraction is necessarily linked to those functions.

The other choices either contradict the evidence (A wrongly predicts solitary newborns find it threatening; D addresses untested adult comparisons) or reverse the innate/learned distinction the evidence supports (C).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
